acceptance.criterion: Normal flow, actor authority, state transitions, wake conditions, proof rules, and terminal outcomes are defined once.
Every retained safety-kernel requirement has a canonical destination and a proving test.
Risk is low, standard, or high while trust mode is independently trusted or untrusted.
Claude Code and Codex share identical workflow semantics.
Worker evidence cannot grant merge authority or modify the host-owned landing outcome.
Complete-without-merge and complete-and-merge share one implementation and readiness pipeline.
Active-run land-pr grants driver authority without bypassing or restarting readiness.
Readiness is attested to an exact HEAD and changed inputs invalidate only affected proof.
A host-only merge guard requires completed acceptance, resolved blockers, clean exact-tip review evidence, required checks, and explicit driver authorization.
Trusted full-run await follows a sanitized human-readable worker stream by default with a quiet opt-out.
Follow mode performs no model inference and replaces timed driver chat updates.
Death, hangs, missing or malformed completion, safety violations, explicit blockers, and material scope or assumption changes wake the driver.
Previously pushed trusted worker progress survives recovery and can resume safely from the verified feature tip.
Shared OAuth raw-transcript restrictions and all redaction guarantees remain intact.
Workers commit and push meaningful progress slices with concrete operator-readable subjects.
Native Grok goal mode is used only when capability-proven; otherwise the compatible one-packet fallback is recorded honestly.
Verification follows an explicit changed-surface to affected-consumer to selected-test impact path.
Evidence records its inputs and invalidation scope so unchanged proof is reused.
One cumulative review checks completeness, constitution, declared risks, and concrete regressions.
Blocking findings are consolidated before revision and advisory findings do not delay readiness.
Re-review verifies the revision delta and unresolved blockers without rescanning settled untouched work.
New re-review blockers require a concrete serious regression, acceptance or constitution breach, security or data-integrity issue, or failure introduced by the revision.
Cleanup-only operational changes do not invalidate product proof, while relevant new surfaces broaden proof automatically.
The loop stops on sufficient exact-tip evidence rather than the absence of reviewer suggestions.
SKILL.md is the compact canonical workflow while AGENTS.md is a thin Codex repository adapter rather than a second fork.
README is operator documentation and focused references own runtime, authority, worker, proof, follow, schema, and parity details.
Plans express intent, acceptance, risk, caution, affected surfaces, constitution impacts, focused tests, review focus, dependencies, and optional checkpoints without implementation choreography.
Survival, kickoff, worker, reviewer, validation, landing, and goal templates implement the new fast path without repeating the protocol.
Claude Code aliases and Codex skill or natural-language invocation remain host-honest and behaviorally equivalent.
Codex continuation Goals and Grok Build goal mode are clearly distinct.
Planning and final review may prefer stronger reasoning while monitoring uses no model and lower-effort phase routes remain capability-detected, optional, and prompt-fallback compatible.
Reports, notifications, provider routes, media generation, legacy bounded execution, and untrusted lanes remain useful but outside the normal critical path.
Semantic scenarios prove both terminal outcomes, immutable worker authority, exact-HEAD readiness, delta review convergence, proof reuse, worker failure recovery, and follow-mode behavior.
Claude Code and Codex installed bundles have identical canonical workflow files, schemas, packets, acceptance behavior, and landing policy.
Every old normative requirement is retained, deliberately changed, or explicitly retired in the migration ledger with a new location and proof.
A fresh independent cumulative review and a Sakana omission/simplification lens find no unresolved blocker.
Targeted tests, release and consistency checks, link and secret checks, installed-bundle smokes, one final broad local suite, and required GitHub CI are green.
Version metadata, README, CHANGELOG, release notes, and Claude Code/Codex installation guidance agree on Elves 2.3.0.

continuation_guard.next_required_action: Finish follow/sentinel revision, delta review, and final readiness

master_acceptance.criterion: A trusted worker can execute the complete plan while the driver stays inactive during healthy progress.
The user can watch worker activity continuously through a default sanitized non-model follow window.
Worker death, hangs, false completion, or genuine safety drift reliably wakes the driver without discarding pushed progress.
Complete-without-merge and complete-and-merge share one implementation, review, revision, and readiness pipeline.
The worker can never authorize or perform merge and the driver merges only with explicit host-owned permission at an attested exact HEAD.
One strong cumulative review plus consolidated fixes and delta-only re-review preserves quality without infinite loops or redundant broad proof.
Claude Code and Codex provide the same workflow, safety, and installed behavior without requiring Grok or any external provider.
No important Elves 2.2 safety invariant is lost in the fresh rewrite.
The normal workflow is materially shorter, quieter, and more fluid than Elves 2.2 while remaining recoverable and trustworthy.
